title: The One Bread in the Boat
subtitle: The Pharisees demand a sign from heaven; the disciples cross the lake having forgotten the bread

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world be left behind.
As the disciples crossed the water, sit now with the one true Bread.
Caught up in Him, they no longer thought of outward bread — be caught up so.
gospel: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Him, tempting Him,
and asked that He show them a sign from heaven.
But He answered and said to them:
'When evening comes you say, It will be fair weather, for the sky is red;
and in the morning, Today there will be a storm, for the sky is red and lowering.
The face of the sky you know how to discern;
can you not know the signs of the times?'
And sighing in His spirit, He said:
'An evil and adulterous generation seeks a sign,
and no sign shall be given it, but the sign of the prophet Jonah.'
And leaving them, He went up again into the boat with His disciples,
And when His disciples had crossed,
they had forgotten to take bread,
and they had with them but one loaf only.
behold: They came to Him not as the devout and the simple, that they might learn,
but as the envious and the scheming, that they might tempt Him.
Some of the Scribes and Pharisees, having let go the study of the Law and the Prophets, gave themselves to astrology;
and by the stars they wished to foretell the coming of Christ — which was impossible.
See the Lord sigh in His spirit:
for as He rejoices over the salvation of men, so He grieves over their errors.
See now the disciples crossing the lake, the worldly din left behind,
having forgotten to take the bread — the fragments of the seven baskets —
and they had with them but one loaf only.
See how inwardly they were ever refreshed in heart,
so that they cared the less for the earthly bread by which the body is fed.
consider: From the signs of the sky, in which you are not taught, you know how to judge the seasons;
how much more from the Scripture of the Law and the Prophets, in which you are taught, ought you to discern that He is the Christ.
This the Lord casts against those who meddle in the deeds of others,
and judge not their own consciences, in which they could read.
'Scarcely will you find one,' says Jerome, 'who, willing to show his own life blameless, would not gladly reproach another's.'
It is said too against those anxious to weigh the coming weather, who will not think upon the hour of their death or the judgment to come.
More praiseworthy, says Augustine, is the soul that knows its own weakness, than the one that searches out the constellations of the heavens and the foundations of the earth.
Evening is a man's old age, morning his youth; the redness is the fervor of charity, the calm the tranquillity of mind.
When in the morning of youth the sky is red, it is the sign of tribulation to come.
But when at the close, at life's end, charity glows red, it is the sign of the serenity that follows the storm.
No sign shall be given but the sign of Jonah — the sign of weakness, the sign of the Passion,
by which every man is helped and consoled.
And the one loaf they had in the boat: mystically it signifies the Lord Himself, the Bread of life, the Saviour;
for the love of whom, being ever refreshed within, they cared less for earthly bread.
So great was the love of the Master that held them, they would not depart from Him even for a moment,
and forgot the very provisions of life, content to have forgotten the bread.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, work in me the sign of Your weakness and Your Passion unto good,
that thereby, through You, I may rejoice to be helped and consoled.
O Bread of life, grant me always to be refreshed by Your love,
that I may be less anxious for bread or for any temporal comfort.
Grant that I may beware of the leaven of perverse doctrine, of feigned justice, and of evil deed,
and that in these I may offend You neither actively nor passively.
O Splendor of the Father's glory, illumine my heart with the light of Your divine grace,
and draw me out of all errors and snares,
and direct me into the way of truth and justice and eternal salvation. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Unus panis quem secum habebant in navi, mystice ipsum panem vitæ Dominum, videlicet Salvatorem designat; cujus amore, quia semper intus reficiebantur in corde, minus de terreno pane curabant.
latinJewel.english: The one loaf they had with them in the boat mystically signifies the Lord Himself, the Bread of life, the Saviour; for the love of whom, because they were ever refreshed within in their heart, they cared the less for earthly bread.
